author: y4rr
content:
Companion to qnd-trial-intel-rs#5 (umbrella qnd-trial-intel-rs#3). Phase 0b deliverable for the Rust port of qnd-trial-intel.

## Why
qnd-trial-intel currently runs meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct via a separate vllm serve process. The Rust rewrite replaces that with embedded ferrotorch-llama inference. Today ferrotorch-llama ships an 8B example only.

## Scope
1. LlamaConfig presets for Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct:
   - 80 hidden layers
   - 64 attention heads
   - 8 key-value heads (GQA)
   - hidden_size 8192, intermediate_size 28 672
   - vocab 128 256
   - RoPE theta 500 000
   - context length 128 000
2. Weight loader for the 70B HF safetensors layout in ferrotorch-serialize.
3. examples/llama3_70b_gpu.rs that loads weights and runs greedy generation under --features cuda.
4. Quantization path so 70B fits on a single 8-GPU node. The Python pyproject.toml gpu extra pins bitsandbytes and compressed-tensors — we need at least one equivalent format. Suggested: GGUF Q4/Q5 via ferrotorch-serialize, or a compressed-tensors-equivalent.

## Acceptance (mirrors qnd-trial-intel-rs AC-17)
- Held-out prompt set of >=10 prompts: temperature=0 greedy completions byte-equal to a HuggingFace transformers reference run (fp16/bf16 baseline).
- Quantized variant passes within a configurable perplexity tolerance vs. the fp16 baseline.

## Consumer
qnd-trial-intel-rs/qnd-trial-intel-llm wraps the Llama model + ferrotorch-paged + ferrotorch-tokenize into an LlmEngine. See .design/qnd-trial-intel-rust-port.md REQ-9 / REQ-18 for full context.
